item.text: I’ll read the finding and inspect the referenced code paths before editing, then keep the fix scoped to the vulnerability without changing intended behavior.

item.text: The finding points at API handlers with missing authorization. I’m going to inspect the route wiring, auth middleware, and existing authz tests so the remediation uses the project’s current access model.

Some of the listed handlers already contain per-object or admin checks, so I’m checking whether the issue is inconsistent enforcement on specific routes or missing coverage around list/filter helpers.

item.aggregated_output:
op2 := &models.Operator{
		ID:           uuid.New().String(),
		Username:     "other-user",
		PasswordHash: "hash",
		Role:         "user",
		Status:       models.OperatorStatusActive,
		AuthProvider: models.OperatorAuthLocal,
	}
	err := srv.store.CreateOperator(context.Background(), op2)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	ctx := context.Background()
	ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, actorContextKey, op2)

	// Try to access network in other operator's CA
	network := &models.Network{
		ID:   uuid.New().String(),
		Name: "test-network",
		CAID: ca.ID,
	}

	ok, err := srv.canAccessNetwork(ctx, network)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, ok, "non-owner should not have access to network in other's CA")
	_ = op1 // prevent unused warning
}

item.text: The codebase already has a CA ownership model. One risky gap remains in helper/list paths: they assume every object has a CA owner and sometimes dereference the actor implicitly, so I’m going to tighten that into shared fail-closed helpers and add regression tests for the listed endpoints.

item.text: There are already endpoint tests for most of the object-level operations. I’m going to add the missing hardening in code first, then use focused tests to prove non-admins can’t see or mutate outside their scope.

item.aggregated_output: internal/api/operators_admin_test.go:20:func createUserWithAPIKey(t *testing.T, srv *Server, role string) string {
/bin/bash: line 1: go: command not found

item.text: Before editing, I found `go` is not installed in this container, so I won’t be able to run the Go test suite here. I’ll still add compile-conscious, narrowly scoped changes and report that verification limitation clearly.

I added shared authorization helpers that fail closed on missing actors and centralize CA ownership scoping. Next I’m wiring the affected handlers to those helpers so list and object paths use one consistent rule.

The CA handlers now avoid implicit actor dereferences and use the shared CA access check. I’m applying the same ownership-set helper to host and network listing so scoping can’t drift between endpoints.

item.command: /bin/bash -lc 'rg "audit log access|operator management requires|network creation requires|blocklist access|admin role required|enforce_2fa" internal/api/*_test.go -n'
/bin/bash -lc 'rg "audit log access|operator management requires|network creation requires|blocklist access|admin role required|enforce_2fa" internal/api/*_test.go -n'

item.text: I’m also moving the admin-only endpoints behind route-level admin middleware while keeping the handler checks. That gives defense in depth at the routing boundary without changing which callers are allowed.
The route table now enforces admin role before admin-only handlers are invoked. I’m doing a static pass for stale call sites and formatting/compile issues since the Go toolchain isn’t available here.

item.text: One detail: route-level blocking would otherwise standardize the error message for all admin-only routes. I’m adjusting the middleware to preserve each endpoint family’s existing 403 message, so the permission behavior is hardened without changing response text.
The admin middleware now accepts the existing endpoint message. I’m splitting the admin routes by endpoint family so current response bodies remain stable.

- [internal/api/cas.go](/work/internal/api/cas.go), [internal/api/hosts.go](/work/internal/api/hosts.go), [internal/api/networks.go](/work/internal/api/networks.go): wired handlers to the shared authorization helpers and removed duplicated scoping logic.
